backbone_sources: SC Residential Property Condition Disclosure Statement (SCR230) - 8 disclosure areas
InterNACHI International Standards of Practice 3.1-3.10 - inspection coverage
design_rules: General and blind: questions derive from national/state standards, not any specific property.
Presence first (does the home have it), then condition (assess what's present), then seller-knowledge disclosures, then constraints.
Photos pre-fill presence and a condition draft; questions confirm and fill what photos cannot see.
Any scope-unknown condition answered 'unsure' resolves to 'inspect', never a fabricated cost.
Seller language only. No component_ids shown to the seller.
Cosmetic upgrades (dated fixtures, pulls, paint, curb appeal) are captured as discretionary 'upgrade' items, not defects. They are among the highest-ROI seller moves and must be offered, never Floor.
No question prompt contains 'only if you have one'. Presence gates reveal child questions. Where a gate is imperfect, the answer set includes a real 'Doesn't apply' option instead of caveat text.

presence_questions.prompt: Roughly how old is the roof, and is it the original roof?
What does the home sit on?
Is there a deck, raised porch, or balcony?
Is there a front porch or exterior stairs with railings?
Is there a garage with a powered door?
Are there gutters on the home?
Roughly how old is the heating and cooling system?
Roughly how old is the water heater?
How many bathrooms, and which is the primary?
Is the home on public water and sewer, or a well and/or septic system?

condition_questions.prompt: Any roof leaks, water stains on ceilings, or missing/curling shingles?
In the crawlspace or basement, is there standing water, dampness, a musty smell, or visible cracks?
Is the siding damaged, rotted, or showing gaps where water could get in?
On the deck, are any boards soft or rotted, railings loose, or gaps between stair risers?
options: soft boards / loose rail / open risers

condition_questions.prompt: On porch or exterior stairs, are handrails loose or missing, or steps uneven?
Does the heating and cooling both run and keep the home comfortable?
Any active leaks, low water pressure, slow drains, or a water heater that leaks or runs out fast?
Near the panel or in the attic, do you see exposed wires, open boxes without covers, or missing outlet/switch cover plates? (Visible signs only.)
condition_questions.note: Hidden junction boxes are not seller-visible. This catches visible signs; an inspection catches the rest. Confidence flagged lower without inspection.

condition_questions.prompt: Are there working smoke detectors on every level, and a carbon monoxide detector?
Are outdoor electrical outlets missing cover plates or not GFCI-protected?
Any cracked panes, windows that won't open/lock, or broken seals (foggy glass)?
condition_questions.note: Cracked pane is floor-eligible only in a safety-glazing location (door, stairwell, wet area); otherwise discretionary.

condition_questions.prompt: Does the garage door open and close normally on its own power?
Are any gutters clogged, sagging, or pulling away from the fascia?
Does the fireplace work, and has the chimney/flue been serviced?
Inside, is there heavy odor (smoke/pet), staining, or damage to walls/ceilings?
condition_questions.note: Heavy smoke/odor triggers REM-01 (remediation) and DUCT-01 (duct cleaning) together.

condition_questions.prompt: Is the flooring worn, damaged, or dated enough that you'd expect a buyer to want it redone?
Is the kitchen dated or worn (cabinets, counters, fixtures)? This is your judgment, not a defect.
condition_questions.note: Datedness is a value/discretionary judgment vision can't make; ask the seller.

condition_questions.prompt: Are the bathrooms dated, or is anything loose, leaking, or pulling away from the wall (vanity, toilet, tile)?
condition_questions.note: Catches the dated-and-separating-vanity case vision misses.

disclosure_questions.prompt: Any known problems with water supply, well, septic, or sewer?
Any known roof, foundation, or structural problems, past or present?
Any known problems with plumbing, electrical, heating, or cooling systems?
Any known termite or wood-destroying insect activity or damage?
Any known environmental issues: mold, asbestos, lead paint, radon, methamphetamine production, or other contamination?
disclosure_questions.note: SC disclosure explicitly lists these; meth/mold/lead can be sale-blocking and disclosure-mandatory.

disclosure_questions.prompt: Is the property in an HOA or subject to covenants or restrictions? If so, are there dues or pending assessments?
disclosure_questions.note: Drives the HOA estoppel/transfer fee in net proceeds.

constraints_questions.prompt: Roughly what's left on your primary mortgage?
Any second mortgage or HELOC balance? (Enter 0 if none.)
Any liens, unpaid property taxes, or contractor bills against the home? (Enter 0 if none.)
Could you pay for pre-listing repairs up front?
Would you be prepared to finance the repairs (loan or credit)?
How soon do you need to sell?
Do you already expect to offer any buyer credits or concessions? (Optional, enter 0 if none.)
Any other seller costs you want included (moving, staging, extra attorney work)? (Optional.)

_branch_targets.floor_candidate_safety: Stage as safety-eligible Floor candidate; requires explicit seller confirmation before writing to Floor.
_branch_targets.floor_candidate_lender: Stage as lender-eligible Floor candidate; requires explicit seller confirmation.
_branch_targets.floor_candidate_safety_lender: Stage as both; requires explicit seller confirmation.
_branch_targets.floor_candidate_safety_lender_replace: Stage as Floor candidate AND set default action to replace (failed component), pending confirmation.
_branch_targets.remediation_and_duct_floor: Stage REM-01 and DUCT-01 together as essential-when-detected Floor candidates, pending confirmation.
_branch_targets.inspect: Resolve to 'inspect' action: show as 'get this checked before deciding', exclude from plan cost totals, do not fabricate a cost.
_branch_targets.strong_recommendation_mode: Seller can neither pay nor finance: switch output to Floor-only strong recommendation framing.
_branch_targets.constraints_note: Never present payoff/constraints as costs 'coming out of the sale'. They size feasibility, not deductions from a number the seller sees as theirs.
_branch_targets.upgrade_candidate: Stage as a DISCRETIONARY upgrade (work_type=upgrade), not a defect and never Floor. Priced as a low-cost refresh with its own recoup %. Seller chooses whether to include.

cosmetic_upgrade_questions.prompt: Kitchen: are the cabinet pulls, faucet, and light fixtures dated or builder-basic? (Cheap to refresh, often high return.)
Bathrooms: are the faucets, lighting, mirror, and hardware dated or builder-basic?
Throughout the home, are light fixtures dated (brass, bowl lights, etc.)?
Are interior wall colors dated or bold enough that neutral paint would help it show better?
Are interior door handles and cabinet/door hardware dated or mismatched?
Curb appeal: would the entry, paint, or landscaping benefit from a low-cost refresh (front door color, mulch, pressure wash)?
